name: upmark
description: Present markdown documents to a human reader in upmark, with interactive task lists that flow back to the LLM.
long_description:
upmark is a native markdown reader for people who'd rather *read* their notes than fight a kitchen-sink editor. This extension lets MCP clients that don't speak localhost SSE (Claude Desktop, Claude Code) talk to upmark's MCP server through a stdio bridge.

The bridge auto-launches upmark if it isn't already running, then proxies tool calls — `present_document` pushes a document into the reader, `get_document_status` reads back which task-list items the user has checked, and so on.

upmark itself must be installed separately from <https://github.com/captured-ventures/upmark/releases>. The bridge searches Program Files, /Applications, and PATH; set `UPMARK_BIN` to override.

upmark_bin.title: upmark binary path
upmark_bin.description: Override the path to the upmark binary. Leave blank to auto-detect (Program Files on Windows, /Applications on macOS, PATH on Linux).

upmark_mcp_port.title: MCP server port
upmark_mcp_port.description: Port upmark exposes the MCP server on. Leave at 11451 unless you've changed it in upmark's settings.

tools.name: present_document
tools.description: Display a markdown document in upmark for the user to read and interact with. Returns the document id, which can be passed to get_document_status to poll for the user's task-list selections, or to update_document to revise the content.

tools.name: update_document
tools.description: Replace the content of a previously-presented document. Task-list selections that match by line text are preserved; new tasks start unchecked.

tools.name: get_document_status
tools.description: Return the current status of a presented document, including which task-list items the user has checked, whether the doc is currently in view, and whether the user has explicitly closed it.

tools.name: close_document
tools.description: Remove a presented document from upmark.

tools.name: list_presented
tools.description: List all documents currently presented in upmark.
